test_cases.name: happy_path_standard_project
test_cases.scenario: Generate a complete communication plan for a standard mid-size project with clear organizational structure and defined timeline.

input.project_scope: 12-month digital transformation initiative affecting 5 departments with goal of modernizing customer-facing systems and improving operational efficiency.
input.organizational_structure: Executive steering committee (CEO, CFO, CTO), Program management office (Program Manager, Communications Lead), Technical team (10 engineers), Business analysts (3), Customer success team (5), Finance team (4)
input.project_charter: Strategic initiative to reduce customer onboarding time by 50% and improve system uptime to 99.9%. Budget: $2M. Timeline: Jan-Dec 2024.
input.organizational_communication_norms: Formal written communication for executive updates, collaborative meetings for technical discussions, email for routine updates, monthly all-hands meetings.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill executes all 7 phases successfully, producing a complete stakeholder communication plan with at least 6 distinct stakeholder groups, tailored messages for each, assigned communication channels, defined cadence, templates, and integrated plan document.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Identify Stakeholders
Phase 2: Analyze Stakeholder Needs and Interests
Phase 3: Design Tailored Messages
Phase 4: Select Communication Channels
Phase 5: Establish Communication Cadence and Risk Assessment
Phase 6: Create Reusable Templates and Crisis Protocol
Phase 7: Design Engagement Tracking and Validate Plan

test_cases.name: happy_path_with_cultural_context
test_cases.scenario: Generate communication plan for a global project with multi-region stakeholders and linguistic diversity considerations.

input.project_scope: Global cloud migration affecting operations in North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific regions with 8-month timeline.
input.organizational_structure: Regional leadership teams (NA, EMEA, APAC), Central IT team, Local IT teams per region, Business unit heads, End-user representatives

cultural_norms: Direct communication preferred in North America
Accommodate time zone differences in scheduling

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill identifies regional stakeholder variations, adapts messages for cultural norms and linguistic clarity, ensures channel accessibility across regions and languages, accommodates time zone differences in scheduling.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Identify Stakeholders
Phase 2: Analyze Stakeholder Needs and Interests
Phase 3: Design Tailored Messages
Phase 4: Select Communication Channels
Phase 5: Establish Communication Cadence and Risk Assessment
Phase 6: Create Reusable Templates and Crisis Protocol
Phase 7: Design Engagement Tracking and Validate Plan

test_cases.name: edge_case_minimal_stakeholders
test_cases.scenario: Generate communication plan for a small project with minimal stakeholder groups (exactly 5 groups at quality gate boundary).

input.organizational_structure: Project sponsor (VP Operations), Project manager, Core team (2 people), Finance contact, Executive stakeholder

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill successfully generates plan with exactly 5 stakeholder groups (meeting minimum quality gate), produces tailored messages and channels for each group despite small scale.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Identify Stakeholders
Phase 2: Analyze Stakeholder Needs and Interests
Phase 3: Design Tailored Messages
Phase 4: Select Communication Channels
Phase 5: Establish Communication Cadence and Risk Assessment

test_cases.name: edge_case_uncertain_timeline
test_cases.scenario: Generate communication plan when project timeline is uncertain and subject to change, requiring relative timing instead of fixed dates.

input.project_scope: Research and development initiative with uncertain completion date.
input.organizational_structure: Research director, Research team (5), Executive sponsor, Board representative

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill uses relative timing (e.g., 'within 1 week of milestone', 'upon phase completion') instead of fixed dates, documents uncertainty in schedule, enables plan to adapt as timeline becomes clearer.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Identify Stakeholders
Phase 2: Analyze Stakeholder Needs and Interests
Phase 3: Design Tailored Messages
Phase 4: Select Communication Channels
Phase 5: Establish Communication Cadence and Risk Assessment

test_cases.name: error_handling_missing_org_structure
test_cases.scenario: Handle gracefully when organizational structure is unclear or unavailable, proceeding with partial information.

input.organizational_structure: Unknown - please infer from project scope

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill adjusts by inferring stakeholder groups from project scope, documents assumptions and gaps in stakeholder coverage, recommends obtaining org chart for validation, proceeds with available information.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Identify Stakeholders
Phase 2: Analyze Stakeholder Needs and Interests
Phase 3: Design Tailored Messages

test_cases.name: error_handling_risk_register_critical_failure
test_cases.scenario: Handle scenario where risk assessment identifies critical failure modes with no viable mitigation strategy.

input.project_scope: High-risk infrastructure migration with potential for complete system failure.
input.organizational_structure: CTO, Infrastructure team, Operations team, Executive steering committee

risk_register.risk: Complete system failure during migration window
risk_register.impact: Business operations halted, customer impact, revenue loss

risk_register.risk: Stakeholder refusal to engage with communication plan

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill identifies critical failure modes, documents mitigation strategies and contingency actions, escalates unmitigable risks to project sponsor, notes in communication plan that critical risks require executive decision-making.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Identify Stakeholders
Phase 2: Analyze Stakeholder Needs and Interests
Phase 5: Establish Communication Cadence and Risk Assessment

test_cases.name: quality_gate_power_interest_grid_application
test_cases.scenario: Verify that influence and interest levels are assigned using Power/Interest Grid with explicit criteria, not subjective judgment.

input.project_scope: Enterprise software implementation affecting all departments.
input.organizational_structure: CEO (can block project), CFO (controls budget), CTO (technical authority), Department heads (affected by change), End-users (directly impacted)
input.project_charter: Strategic initiative to standardize enterprise systems. Budget: $5M. Timeline: 18 months.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill assigns influence/interest levels using explicit Power/Interest Grid criteria (e.g., 'High Influence = can block project'), documents assessment rationale for each group, produces matrix with clear categorization (Manage Closely, Keep Satisfied, Keep Informed, Monitor).
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Identify Stakeholders
Phase 2: Analyze Stakeholder Needs and Interests

test_cases.name: quality_gate_crisis_protocol_specificity
test_cases.scenario: Verify that crisis communication protocol defines triggers with specific, measurable conditions, not ambiguous language.

input.project_scope: Critical infrastructure project with high visibility and tight timeline.
input.organizational_structure: Project director, Technical leads, Executive sponsor, Board members, Regulatory contacts

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill defines crisis triggers with specific, measurable conditions (e.g., 'delay > 2 weeks' not 'significant delay'), specifies notification timelines (e.g., 'within 4 hours'), assigns responsible parties, provides rapid-response templates for each scenario.
phase_coverage: Phase 5: Establish Communication Cadence and Risk Assessment
Phase 6: Create Reusable Templates and Crisis Protocol

test_cases.name: quality_gate_engagement_tracking_measurability
test_cases.scenario: Verify that engagement tracking metrics are measurable and aligned with communication objectives, with specific target thresholds.

input.project_scope: Customer-facing product launch with need to track stakeholder engagement.
input.organizational_structure: Product manager, Marketing team, Sales team, Customer support, Executive leadership

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill defines engagement metrics with specific target thresholds (e.g., delivery rate > 95%, engagement rate > 70%), specifies measurement methods (email open rates, meeting attendance, survey responses), defines review frequency (weekly for high-influence groups, monthly for others).
phase_coverage: Phase 7: Design Engagement Tracking and Validate Plan

test_cases.name: quality_gate_template_completeness_and_customization
test_cases.scenario: Verify that templates are created for each primary channel and stakeholder group with clear customization guidance.

input.project_scope: Multi-phase transformation project requiring diverse communication approaches.
input.organizational_structure: Executives, Technical teams, Business teams, End-users, Vendors
input.organizational_communication_norms: Email for routine updates, meetings for decisions, dashboards for metrics, presentations for major announcements

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill creates at least one template for each primary channel (email, meeting, report, dashboard, presentation), provides templates for each stakeholder group, includes placeholders for project-specific data, documents customization guidance and approval process, creates variations for different message types (routine, risk, milestone, change).
phase_coverage: Phase 6: Create Reusable Templates and Crisis Protocol

phases.phase: Phase 2: Analyze Stakeholder Needs and Interests
Phase 5: Establish Communication Cadence and Risk Assessment
Phase 6: Create Reusable Templates and Crisis Protocol
Phase 7: Design Engagement Tracking and Validate Plan

quality_gates.gate: Phase 1: At least 5 distinct stakeholder groups identified
Phase 2: Power/Interest Grid criteria applied with documented rationale
Phase 3: Messages tailored with cultural adaptations if applicable
Phase 5: Communication risks assessed with mitigation and contingency strategies
Phase 6: Crisis protocol with specific, measurable triggers
Phase 7: Engagement metrics measurable with specific target thresholds

error_handlers.error: Critical failure modes with no viable mitigation
